dataList.dlg: Utter to me what you think the ideal is.
Break the birdcage,
And be free.
I burn what I wish to burn.
It will… turn all to ash.
If it is fortune you seek—pray with all sincerity.
Speak unto me your wish… Vocalize your eagerness…
Such enchanting colors… The path is intent on thwarting all attempts to memorize it.
As a matter of fact, the alley is an open one.
I worry that this seeping rain may leak through the umbrella.
The air is too cold... and raindrops only grow thicker.
This is a portrait of theirs. Will you observe... a little closer?
Leave at once, interloper! Leave this place...!

I shall feel the ponderous mass of the gun barrel rest upon my viscera.
Let this accursed bullet pierce through them...!
There is neither; no fragments of memories, no need to look into them... fire, only fire!
There is neither; no fragments of memories, no need to look into them... fire, only fire!

Fracture the present a thousandfold. Fracture the futures cast upon the shards yet another thousandfold. At long last, the ruined past shall finally lie shattered, the constitution of the universe in the wake of a singular, inevitable circumvolution—Trisāhasramahāsāhasra.
Fracture the present a thousandfold (一千).
Fracture the futures cast upon the shards yet another thousandfold (二千).
At long last, the ruined past shall finally lie shattered,

the constitution of the universe in the wake of a singular,
inevitable circumvolution—Trisāhasramahāsāhasra (三千大天)!

May the manifold worlds of five hundred thousand
nayuta asaṃkhyeya be dyed anew.

Within them shall I come to visit me,
who has been broken into kalapas,
whose immeasurable kalpas of the future have been shattered.
The histories of myself, ground into dust, shall, at last, scatter... and meet annihilation.
The First Death Knell. I come bearing an obituary, for your funeral pyre shall come alight in the grievous fusillade of butterflies! ... And in its wake, naught shall remain.
For the dead, the first bell tolls.
I come bearing an obituary,
For your funeral pyre shall come alight in the grievous fusillade of butterflies!
... And in its wake, naught shall remain.
For the dead, the first bell tolls.

The proximal and distal points cross the arc,
a lepidopteran proboscis transfixing the umstroke of nectaries.
And at the committal,
My linear self shall bear the circle to rest.
